fault_hypothesis: Lines 76-77 contain a spurious post-processing step that pops the last element when both inputs share the same final value, incorrectly removing a valid duplicate that should be retained in the merged result.

constraints.intent: This pop() call should never execute; it incorrectly removes a valid duplicate from the merged result.
The branch that pops the last merged element should never be taken; any entry into this branch indicates a bug.
Before returning, the merged list length must equal the sum of both input lengths since all elements should be preserved.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
